content:
Here are concise, natural ways to handle both possibilities:  

---

### **If she agrees**
> “Thank you — I appreciate that. I’ll keep the recording private and use it only to review the discussion later.”

*(Begin recording, making sure her verbal consent is captured at the start.)*

---

### **If she declines**
> “No problem at all. I’ll just take a few notes instead so I can remember the key points.”

*(Then continue normally; jot brief timestamps or short bullet notes on your iPad.)*

---

Both lines keep things calm, professional, and unambiguous. Would you like me to show you a clean way to set up the iPad so you can record or take notes hands-free during the call, depending on which way she answers?
